choice_filling_tips.title: Fill Maximum Choices
choice_filling_tips.description: There's no limit to choices. Fill at least 100-150 options to maximize your chances. More choices = more safety net.

choice_filling_tips.title: Follow Safe-Target-Dream Strategy
choice_filling_tips.description: Maintain a balanced mix: 40% Safe options (high probability), 40% Target options (realistic), 20% Dream options (ambitious).

choice_filling_tips.title: Order Matters Strictly
choice_filling_tips.description: The system allocates the FIRST available choice from top. Never put a less preferred college above a more preferred one.

choice_filling_tips.title: Branch vs College Dilemma
choice_filling_tips.description: For CSE/IT: Branch > College. For core branches: College brand matters more. Consider long-term career goals.

choice_filling_tips.title: Don't Ignore Home State Quota
choice_filling_tips.description: Home state quota significantly improves your chances. Always include home state colleges in your list.

choice_filling_tips.title: Research Before Filling
choice_filling_tips.description: Check college placements, infrastructure, faculty, and alumni reviews. Don't rely only on rankings.

common_mistakes.impact: High risk of not getting any seat
common_mistakes.solution: Always include safe options. Better to have a good college than no college.
Analyze 3-year cutoff trends. Cutoffs can vary but patterns exist.
Research each branch thoroughly. Consider your interests and aptitude.
Don't follow trends blindly. Make informed decisions based on data.
Float if you want to upgrade, Freeze only if 100% satisfied with current seat.

josaa_explanation.what: Joint Seat Allocation Authority - centralized admission process for IITs, NITs, IIITs, and GFTIs
process: Register on JoSAA portal with JEE credentials
Fill and lock choices (can modify between rounds)
Seat allocation based on rank and preferences
Report to college for document verification
important_points: Seat allocation is done in order of your choice list
You can only get one seat per round
Freezing a seat means you exit counselling
Floating allows you to look for better options in next rounds

csab_explanation.what: Central Seat Allocation Board - special rounds after JoSAA for leftover seats in NIT+ system
csab_explanation.difference_from_josaa: CSAB only includes NITs, IIITs, and GFTIs. IIT seats are NOT part of CSAB.
who_can_participate: Students who didn't get any seat in JoSAA
Students who want to upgrade from their JoSAA seat
Fresh candidates who didn't participate in JoSAA
csab_explanation.risk: CSAB is riskier as fewer seats available. Only participate if you're willing to take calculated risk.

faqs.question: What is the difference between CRL and Category Rank?
faqs.answer: CRL (Common Rank List) is your All India Rank. Category Rank is your rank within your category (OBC/SC/ST/EWS). For seat allocation, category rank is used for reserved seats.

faqs.question: Can I get a seat if my rank is slightly above closing rank?
faqs.answer: It's possible but risky. Cutoffs vary by 5-10% each year. If your rank is within 5% of closing rank, you have a chance in later rounds or CSAB.

faqs.question: Should I choose IIT lower branch or NIT top branch?
faqs.answer: Depends on your goals. For placements and coding career: NIT CSE > IIT lower branch. For research and brand value: IIT > NIT.

faqs.question: What is Float, Freeze, and Slide?
faqs.answer: Float: Keep current seat but look for ANY better option. Freeze: Accept seat and exit counselling. Slide: Keep current seat but look for better BRANCH in SAME college.

faqs.question: Can I participate in state counselling after JoSAA?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can participate in both. But if you accept a JoSAA seat, you may need to pay fees for both initially.
